vulnerability.title: The Installed Operating System Is Vendor Supported
vulnerability.description: The installed operating system must be maintained by a vendor. Red Hat Enterprise Linux is supported by Red Hat, Inc. As the Red Hat Enterprise Linux vendor, Red Hat, Inc. is responsible for providing security patches.
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: The Installed Operating System Is Vendor Supported

triage_decision.reason: Ensuring the OS is vendor supported involves checking subscription status and enabling appropriate repositories, which does not modify authentication, security-critical files, or filesystem structures in a way that could cause lockout or break services.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; May require network access to subscription manager

review_feedback: The plan enables repositories but does not change the underlying OS to a vendor-supported distribution; Rocky Linux is not vendor-supported by Red Hat, so the vulnerability would remain.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan enables repositories but does not change the underlying OS to a vendor-supported distribution; Rocky Linux is not vendor-supported by Red Hat, so the vulnerability would remain.
concerns: Rocky Linux is a community rebuild, not vendor-supported by Red Hat.
Enabling repo files does not confer vendor support status.
suggested_improvements: Replace the OS with a vendor-supported distribution such as Red Hat Enterprise Linux.
If using RHEL, ensure the system is registered with Red Hat Subscription Manager and has appropriate subscriptions.
Verify vendor support status via subscription-manager or equivalent before considering the vulnerability resolved.
